<findings>

[observation] [info] Add structured logging/metrics inside `tools/files.ts`, `tools/git.ts`, and `tools/shell.ts` so production incidents can trace which guard blocked an action.
[observation] [info] Augment `guard/policy.ts` with scenario-based tests for forbidden operations to ensure policy edits don’t silently weaken protections.
[observation] [info] Define an explicit boundary package (e.g., `agent-tools`) so `hench` and `hench-2` no longer require 18 mutual imports, keeping responsibilities clearer.

</findings>

<insights>

- Clarify the guard-to-agent API to reduce the 10↔8 bidirectional imports with `hench` and avoid accidental cycles.
- Expand tests in `guard/policy.ts` to cover negative cases so high-risk commands get rejected deterministically.
- Instrument the tool wrappers (`tools/files.ts`, `tools/git.ts`, `tools/shell.ts`) for better observability when guards intervene.
- Define an explicit boundary package (e.g., `agent-tools`) so `hench` and `hench-2` no longer require 18 mutual imports, keeping responsibilities clearer.
- Augment `guard/policy.ts` with scenario-based tests for forbidden operations to ensure policy edits don’t silently weaken protections.
- Add structured logging/metrics inside `tools/files.ts`, `tools/git.ts`, and `tools/shell.ts` so production incidents can trace which guard blocked an action.
- [call graph] 198 internal calls, 27 outgoing, 2 incoming (cohesion: 0.88, coupling: 0.12)

</insights>
